valid.name: inner-join-on
valid.code: SELECT 1 FROM a JOIN b ON a.id = b.id;

valid.name: inner-join-using
valid.code: SELECT 1 FROM a INNER JOIN b USING (id);

invalid.name: cross-join
invalid.code: SELECT 1 FROM a CROSS JOIN b;

errors.message: Avoid `CROSS JOIN`. Cartesian products are almost always a mistake; use an explicit `JOIN ... ON` with a join condition, or `JOIN ... ON true` if you really do want one.
